h1: Traditional or Modern? Find Your Perfect Style
h2: Traditional or Modern Fireplace? How We Chose the Right Look for Our New Construction Home
How to Get the Modern Farmhouse Living Room Look

paragraphs: Whether you’re building a new construction home or transforming your existing house into dream-home status, many people are making significant home investments, prioritizing features that add comfort and make a house feel like a sanctuary.

Installing a fireplace is often at the top of the to-do list—and for good reason. In addition to providing an incomparable focal point, a fireplace adds warmth and sophistication like no other feature can. Hearths truly are the heart of our homes, bringing families and friends together.
Explore different design styles and a variety of fireplace options by visiting the Heat & Glo Inspiration Gallery.

With their ornate faces, decorative mantels, arched lintels and classic firebrick, a traditional wood-burning hearth is what most people imagine when they hear the word “fireplace.” We have a natural attraction to the traditional concept of burning wood in a fireplace—but a wood-burning hearth isn’t always feasible (or affordable) for every home.

That’s where traditional gas fireplaces come in: Super-realistic logs and natural stone or wood surrounds provide all the things we love about wood-burning fireplaces, but in a much more convenient, nearly maintenance-free package.

While traditional fireplaces emulate what we love about wood-burning hearths, modern gas models completely reinvent the design wheel. Essential to this style are clean lines, unique fire beds and flames that are as stylish as they are warm. Similar to how we display art, modern hearths often are positioned higher on the wall, even at eye level.

Fireplaces with a modern flair offer the same benefits as traditional gas fireplaces—such as Wi-fi or touchscreen controls and heat management technology—but with sleek looks that fit seamlessly into a more contemporary home.
Visit the Heat & Glo Inspiration Gallery to find these fireplaces and other design ideas.
Planning to add a hearth to your home? Here’s what it costs to purchase, install and run a fireplace—whether it’s gas, electric or wood-burning.
Blogger and new home buyer Rachael Dorr walks us through her fireplace selection process.
See how one interior designer makes her client’s modern farmhouse dreams come true, putting fireplaces center stage in living rooms of every size.
Browse our photo gallery to find a fireplace design that reflects your unique style.
